questions.question: Which state/UT is the first to provide the first caste certificate based on the mother’s credentials?
questions.explanation: Delhi’s Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia handed over the first caste certificate based on the mother’s credentials to a single mother. Earlier, SC/ST caste certificates were issued only based on the fathers’ caste certificates or paternal side certificates. Hence, this is considered as a landmark move

questions.question: Which country recently fired a “hypersonic missile”, after its first test in September?
questions.explanation: North Korea recently fired a “hypersonic missile” that successfully hit a target. The test-fire was detected by several militaries in the region, and drew criticism from the United States, South Korea, and Japan. North Korea first tested a hypersonic missile in September. Hypersonic weapons can achieve more than five times the speed of sound – or about 6,200 km per hour.

questions.question: India’s first RT-PCR kit to detect the Omicron variant, named ‘Omisure’ is developed by which company?
questions.explanation: India has recently approved a new RT-PCR test kit that can detect whether a sample is Covid-19 positive and if the variant is Omicron. The kit, called Omisure has been developed by TataMD, in partnership with ICMR. At present, the variant requires genome sequencing which can be done by specialist, large laboratories, for multiple days. It detects Sars-CoV-2, and it has two specific targets for the S gene that are Omicron-specific.

questions.question: The Koppal Toy Cluster (KTC), which was seen in the news recently, is being developed in which state?
questions.explanation: Koppal is a town in the district of Koppal in Karnataka. The Koppal Toy Cluster (KTC), which is being developed in the state, is the first such infrastructure in the country. The Toy Cluster will be operational in March 2022, spanning an area of over 400 acres of land. It will include the complete value chain from toy making, production of packaging, tool making, paint making, electronics and other accessories.

questions.question: “Teachers’ University Bill” has been passed by the Legislative assembly of which state / UT?
questions.explanation: The Legislative Assembly of Delhi has passed the Delhi Teachers’ University Bill, which aims to build a world-class teachers’ training university at Bakkarwala village. The proposed university would be built on 12 acre a land and will have world class amenities for over 5000 students. The university will offer programmes like BA-BEd and BSc-BEd.

questions.question: India’s first Open Rock Museum is located in which city?
questions.explanation: Union Minister of State for Science and Technology Dr Jitendra Singh inaugurated India’s first Open Rock Museum in Hyderabad. The Museum displays around 35 types of rocks from various parts of India with ages ranging from 3.3 billion years to around 55 million years of the Earth’s history. The Union Minister also released Earthquake Risk Maps of Lucknow and Dehradun, developed by CSIR-National Geophysical Research Institute (NGRI).

questions.question: Sanjay Lake is a wetland located in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: The Asian Waterbird Census (AWC) is being carried out across seven wetlands in the NCR — Hastinapur Wildlife Sanctuary, Dhanauri and Surajpur wetlands in Greater Noida, Delhi Zoo and Sanjay Lake, Okhla Bird Sanctuary, Najafgarh Jheel and the Yamuna River. The census recorded only 13 species in the Sanjay Lake, down from 17 species recorded from 2019 to 2021. The two migratory species recorded were the Great Cormorant, which migrates from Southeast Asia, and the Eurasian Coot, which migrates from Temperate Asia.

questions.question: Which group of countries participate in ‘Sea Dragon 22’ Military Exercise?
questions.explanation: The US Navy announced that the Sea Dragon 22 exercise began along with the navies of India, Australia, Canada, Japan and South Korea. It is a multi-lateral anti-submarine warfare exercise in the Pacific Ocean, participated by six Indo-Pacific nations. India, Japan, Australia and America are also part of the ‘QUAD’ group, and also participate in the Malabar exercise.

questions.question: Which peninsula separates the Gulf of Mexico from the Caribbean Sea?
questions.explanation: The Yucatán Peninsula separates the Gulf of Mexico from the Caribbean Sea. It includes 3 Mexican states and portions of Belize and Guatemala. The Maya civilization, known for its exemplary architecture and understanding of mathematics, originated in the Yucatan Peninsula. According to a new study, the Maya civilization may have had access to nearly 500 edible plants, many of which were highly resistant to drought. Earlier, it was believed that the civilization’s fall was due to drought.

questions.question: India’s first multi-dimensional ‘adventure sports expedition’ was carried out in which country?
questions.explanation: India’s first multi-dimensional ‘adventure sports expedition’ was flagged off on October 27, 2021. It was conducted by National Institute of Mountaineering and Allied Sports (NIMAS) in France. Recently, Defence Minister Rajnath Singh flagged-in India’s first multi-dimensional adventure sports expedition. The team, which included 8 Army personnel and 4 youths of Arunachal Pradesh, carried out over 250 kilometres of winter trekking in the Alps Mountain Ranges, paragliding, 975 kilometres of cycling and 12 deep scuba dives in the Mediterranean Sea.
